channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Blue Jays Rally to Defeat Twins After Valenzuela Homer
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays secured a 10-4 victory over the Minnesota Twins on Friday, overcoming a four-run deficit thanks to a remarkable five-run fourth inning and continued momentum. Rookie Brandon Vale...

item.title: Government and TABOA reach agreement to ensure uninterrupted bus services across Tanzania
item.description: The Tanzania Bus Owners Association (TABOA) has confirmed that bus transport services will continue to operate normally across the country after resolving its dispute with the government. The announce...

item.title: Former Sheriff’s Lieutenant Among Five Charged With Murder in Deadly Esparto Fireworks Explosion
item.description: A former Yolo County Sheriff’s lieutenant, Samuel Machado, has been charged with seven counts of second-degree murder and several other serious offenses after a fireworks explosion killed seven wareho...

item.title: Sindh approves second phase of Karachi Safe City Project to boost urban security and surveillance
item.description: Sindh Chief Minister Syed Murad Ali Shah has given the go-ahead for Phase-II of the Karachi Safe City Project, estimated at Rs9.98 billion, with work expected to start in May 2026 and complete within ...

item.title: Cameron Myers Wins Back-to-Back 1500m National Titles, Nears Australian Record
item.description: Canberra's rising star, Cameron Myers, has once again made headlines by winning back-to-back 1500-metre titles at the Australian Athletics Championships in Sydney. At just 19, Myers clocked an impress...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts Continued Rains Across Several Regions
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department has released a weather forecast predicting continued rainfall across many parts of the country over the next 24 hours. Nairobi and surrounding areas within the High...

item.title: Russian Investigative Journalist Detained Pending Trial
item.description: A Russian court has ordered Oleg Roldugin, an investigative journalist for the independent newspaper Novaya Gazeta, to be held in pre-trial detention until May 10th. This action follows his arrest on ...

item.title: ED investigates Earth Infrastructures Ltd for alleged ₹2,024 crore real estate fraud involving 19,400 buyers
item.description: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has conducted searches at ten locations in Delhi and Gurugram connected with the real estate company Earth Infrastructures Ltd (EIL) and its associates. The agency is ...

item.title: P-Pop group BINI makes history with Coachella debut, fellow artists show support
item.description: Filipino pop culture marked a significant milestone as P-Pop girl group BINI made their debut at the prestigious Coachella music festival. This historic event garnered wide attention from fellow P-Pop...

item.title: EACC report links gender imbalance to higher corruption rates in key industries
item.description: A new survey by Kenya’s Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC) has revealed that corruption levels are notably higher in sectors that are mainly dominated by men. The study looked at different i...

item.title: Okanagan experiences record-low snowpack, raising concerns about potential summer drought
item.description: The Okanagan region in British Columbia is experiencing its lowest snowpack levels in over 40 years, according to data from the B.C. River Forecast Centre. The snow basin index for the Okanagan is cur...

item.title: Baringo Education Director Retires and Celebrates 35-Year Marriage in Church Ceremony
item.description: Julius Kayap from Mogotio, Baringo County, recently marked two significant milestones in his life: retirement from his role as Sub-County Director of Education and his 35th wedding anniversary with hi...

item.title: Rising Diesel and Electricity Costs Threaten Survival of South Africa’s Alluvial Diamond Miners
item.description: South Africa’s alluvial diamond mining sector is under severe pressure as escalating diesel and electricity costs, coupled with declining diamond prices, are forcing many operators to cease operations...

item.title: Kevin Quiambao earns KBL Rookie of the Year, leads Goyang Sono Skygunners to playoffs
item.description: Kevin Quiambao, the talented Filipino basketball star, has been recognized as Rookie of the Year in the Korean Basketball League (KBL) for his remarkable 2025-26 season with the Goyang Sono Skygunners...

item.title: DA faces leadership contest and proposed structural reforms at upcoming congress
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) is preparing for a crucial elective congress this weekend that could significantly alter the party's structure and leadership. Delegates are set to vote on 58 constitution...

item.title: Emotional Tribute by MP David Kiaraho’s Son During His Father’s Funeral
item.description: Ol Kalou Member of Parliament David Kiaraho’s lastborn son, Anthony Ng’ang’a, moved mourners to tears with his heartfelt tribute to his late father. Speaking emotionally during the funeral service, An...

item.title: US and Iran engage in high-stakes negotiations in Pakistan amid Lebanon conflict
item.description: US Vice President J.D. Vance arrived in Islamabad, Pakistan, for critical talks aimed at ending the ongoing six-week conflict with Iran. The US delegation, which includes President Donald Trump's spec...

item.title: Joe Siddall’s path of resilience and reflection as he becomes the Blue Jays’ main analyst
item.description: Joe Siddall, former professional catcher and longtime Blue Jays broadcaster, is starting his first season as the main analyst for Sportsnet’s Blue Jays broadcasts. His journey into broadcasting was bo...

item.title: Japan Buys Crude Oil from Nigeria and Others Amid Middle East Supply Disruption
item.description: Nigeria fit enjoy better revenue soon as Japan don start dey source crude oil from different countries, including Naija, because wahala for Middle East don dey affect oil supply. Japan dey try secure ...

item.title: Adamawa High Court Suspends ADC Party Congress Pending Legal Review
item.description: For Adamawa State, the High Court No. 6 in Yola don issue order to immediately stop all congresses wey the African Democratic Congress (ADC) plan to hold for the state. This one include ward, local go...
